decisions.title: single-card feeder mechanism
decisions.frozen: Core uses one stationary central dealer serving four fixed player seats and five board targets under the robot_core_no_burn dealing profile.
decisions.open: Feeder, separation and selectable face orientation implementation.
decisions.required_evidence: Real-card prototype across multiple decks with no unobserved double-feed.

decisions.title: camera position
decisions.frozen: One fixed camera must observe all thirteen card slots and four fixed player action regions; independent overhead USB is preferred.
decisions.open: Exact device, height, lens, angle and lighting for readable cards and temporally usable player behaviour at all four seats.
decisions.required_evidence: Target-camera samples from all four seat orientations with glyph pixels, glare and occlusion report.

decisions.title: mechanical target geometry
decisions.frozen: Nine target IDs in logical_layout_v1.json: four seats and five board positions; Core has no burn target.
decisions.open: Angles, distances, drop polygons and tolerances.
decisions.required_evidence: Dimensioned tabletop drawing and repeated delivery test per target.

decisions.title: board placement
decisions.frozen: Five ordered board slots with separate mechanical and vision IDs.
decisions.open: Card spacing, physical mat size, delivery trajectory and relation to four showdown areas.
decisions.required_evidence: Four-seat paper mock showing no overlap and complete camera visibility.

decisions.title: showdown exposure
decisions.frozen: Every live player exposes both hole cards into that seat's two fixed ROIs; folded players are not evaluated.
decisions.open: Reveal-order etiquette is not a state dependency; physical ROI geometry remains under S0-02/04.
decisions.required_evidence: Stage 1 multi-player showdown replay and Stage 2 four-orientation usability test.

decisions.title: player action interface
decisions.frozen: Action semantics are fold/check/call/bet/raise with hand/action/state identity and legality validation.
decisions.open: Laptop UI, physical controls, gesture or voice adapters may be substituted; No-Limit would require amount_units for bet/raise.
decisions.required_evidence: Stage 1 adapter-neutral action contract and at least one software UI implementation.

decisions.title: betting structure and numeric defaults
decisions.frozen: Fixed-Limit is the current Core candidate; integer ledger, all-in and multi-player main/side pots are required regardless.
decisions.open: Human product confirmation of Fixed-Limit. 1/2 blinds, 2/4 bets, cap 4 and stack 80 are configurable defaults, not product freeze.
decisions.required_evidence: Product decision followed by versioned Stage 1 reducer config and walkthrough approval.

decisions.title: timeouts and recovery
decisions.frozen: Player timeout defaults to 30 seconds and pauses; mechanism commands are bounded and never imply success.
decisions.open: UI timeout default may change; per-command values require measured hardware P95/P99.
decisions.required_evidence: Stage 3 latency distribution and Stage 4 four-player recovery injection.

decisions.title: sensors and safety
decisions.frozen: Success ACK requires physical evidence; E-stop, homing, interlock and watchdog are mandatory.
decisions.open: Sensor technology, circuit, thresholds, four-seat motion envelope and stop time.
decisions.required_evidence: BOM/schematic plus witnessed home, jam, double-feed, disconnect and E-stop tests.

decisions.title: host-controller protocol
decisions.frozen: Versioned command/ACK semantics, nine target IDs, correlation ID, error codes, idempotency, heartbeat and status query.
decisions.open: USB serial framing, CRC representation and MCU parser constraints.
decisions.required_evidence: Cross-language mock parses valid, invalid, duplicate and interrupted messages.

decisions.title: target decks and lighting
decisions.frozen: A held-out physical deck/session and all four seat orientations are mandatory for final testing.
decisions.open: Exact deck SKUs, count, mat and lighting fixture.
decisions.required_evidence: Procurement list and pilot manifest with at least three physical decks and two face designs.

decisions.title: acceptance duration
decisions.frozen: Formal acceptance requires at least 20 consecutive four-player hands; this is a quality gate, not a product feature.
decisions.open: A shorter live presentation may use the same build but cannot replace acceptance evidence.
decisions.required_evidence: Stage 5 hand logs including folds, multi-way showdown, side pot, tie and recovery.

decisions.title: automatic board-card reveal
decisions.frozen: Board cards must end face-up in their fixed slots before visual confirmation; hole cards remain face-down. Core does not deal burn cards.
decisions.open: Selective flip chute, independent reveal board or explicit manual-flip fallback and its impact on product claim.
decisions.required_evidence: Robotics comparison prototype with orientation success, jam and landing-position results.

decisions.title: between-hand reset
decisions.frozen: After every hand an operator collects used and remaining cards, restores a complete deck, shuffles and reloads before homing/ready confirmation.
decisions.open: Exact checklist, deck-count confirmation method and UI prompts.
decisions.required_evidence: Operator walkthrough completing reset without stale software or physical state.

decisions.title: button and turn indication
decisions.frozen: Button, SB, BB, UTG/current actor and seat identities must be unambiguous to all four players.
decisions.open: Laptop-only display, illuminated physical marker or robot lighting implementation.
decisions.required_evidence: Four-player paper/UI walkthrough with no role or action-order ambiguity.

decisions.title: player behaviour perception contract
decisions.frozen: A model observes only the state-machine-selected seat view and emits PlayerActionObservation evidence; optional S0-21 session face verification cannot select the acting seat, and model evidence never mutates the game directly.
decisions.open: Exact gesture grammar, camera coverage, input features, temporal model and whether particular actions require an explicit player confirmation interaction.
decisions.required_evidence: Target-camera pilot covering all five actions, no-action, ordinary hand motion, simultaneous neighbour motion, occlusion and cancellation for all four seats.

decisions.title: acting-seat attention authority
decisions.frozen: The deterministic game state machine is the sole acting-seat authority; only its current seat ROI may yield a candidate action, and attention changes only after a legal action is atomically committed with a new state version.
decisions.open: The visual highlight, sound cue or physical light used to tell players about the already-determined focus remains replaceable under S0-15.
decisions.required_evidence: Stage 1 replay proving non-current-seat and stale-window evidence cannot change state, ledger or focus.

decisions.title: temporal action confirmation and rejection
decisions.frozen: Action promotion requires timestamped multi-frame temporal evidence, calibrated confidence, state-version matching, legality validation and action feedback; ambiguous, occluded, out-of-ROI, conflicting or stale evidence keeps the same acting seat and changes no ledger state.
decisions.open: Per-action thresholds, window duration, entry/exit rules, cooldown and any explicit confirmation step require target-user evidence.
decisions.required_evidence: Held-out participant/session evaluation reporting per-action precision/recall/F1, false accepted actions per hour and hand, rejection coverage and P95 confirmation latency.

decisions.title: phase-aware table scene state
decisions.frozen: The state machine owns each of thirteen slot lifecycles and activates ROIs by expected phase; robot ACK proves dispensing while stable visual evidence separately proves occupancy, face orientation and visible identity. Unknown, occlusion, duplicate identity or conflict cannot advance state.
decisions.open: Final ROI geometry, occupancy/orientation thresholds, board reveal implementation and which hidden-card presence checks are achievable from the target camera.
decisions.required_evidence: Full-hand target-table replay covering empty, face-down, face-up unreadable, confirmed, occluded, duplicate, misplaced and uncleared slot states.

decisions.title: authoritative ledger and audited adjustment
decisions.frozen: The digital ledger is the only Core balance authority; physical chips are neither recognized nor authoritative. Validated actions and explicit audited operator adjustments are the only balance-change sources, applied atomically with state versions and preserved in an append-only hand log.
decisions.open: The operator UI presentation is replaceable; physical chip recognition, collection and payout remain Plus scope.
decisions.required_evidence: Stage 1 property and recovery tests for conservation, idempotency, side-pot eligibility, rebuy or end-session events, and restart from the append-only log.

decisions.title: session-only face identity verification
decisions.frozen: After explicit participant consent and per-session enrollment, local face detection and embeddings may return a registered player_id or unknown for the robot/state-selected seat view. Embeddings remain memory-only, are cleared with the session, and never select acting_seat, transfer cards/chips, or mutate the ledger.
decisions.open: Enrollment UX, detector/embedding admission, cosine and ambiguity thresholds, liveness, target rotating-camera evidence and mismatch recovery.
decisions.required_evidence: Held-out participant/session evaluation covering false match, false non-match, unknown people, multiple faces, pose, lighting, replay/spoof attempts, consent and verified memory-only cleanup.

decisions.title: no-burn robot dealing profile
decisions.frozen: Core v1 uses robot_core_no_burn: no card is burned before Flop, Turn or River; a complete river hand dispenses eight hole cards and five board cards through nine mechanical targets.
decisions.open: None. Reintroducing burn cards requires an explicit product decision and a new dealer protocol version.
decisions.required_evidence: Rules/schema parity, exact no-burn target-order tests, protocol rejection of burn_tray and a thirteen-dispense simulator sequence.
